name: Gingersnap-Rum Baked Pears
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush gingersnap cookies to equal about 1/2 to 3/4 cup crumbs using a blender or food processor.
2. Add half the pecans and pulse until chopped.
3. Toast the remaining pecans (or use untoasted) as garnish.
4. Mix 1 teaspoon melted margarine, 1 teaspoon rum, and 1 teaspoon orange juice.
5. Pulse the mixture 1-2 times only.
6. Wash, peel, halve, and core pears. Place cut side up in a shallow baking dish.
7. Distribute the crumb mixture evenly into each pear cavity.
8. Dot with remaining margarine.
9. Drizzle the remaining rum and orange juice over the filling.
10. Pour hot water to a depth of about 1/4 inch in the baking dish.
11. Place the dish in a preheated 350-degree oven and bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
12. Remove pears with a slotted spoon and place 2 halves on each serving plate.
13. Garnish with remaining pecan halves.
14. Serve with a scoop of vanilla ice cream on each plate, garnished with perfect raspberries if desired.
